R base vs swing.label: R base vs swing
R base vs swing.question: When it comes to the next election, what do you think is more important for Republicans?

options.label: Republicans need to focus on energizing and turning out the base, by running on conservative, America-first policies that give their voters something to vote for
Republicans need to focus on moderating in order to appeal to the swing voters and independents who decide elections
Republicans don’t need to change much from their 2024 strategy

Abortion.label: Abortion
Abortion.question: When it comes to abortion, which of the following is closest to your view?

options.label: Abortion for any reason at any point in pregnancy should be legal
Abortion should be banned after 24 weeks of pregnancy. After 24 weeks, abortion should only be legal in the case of rape, incest, or to save the life of the mother.
Abortion should be banned after 18 weeks of pregnancy. After 18 weeks, abortion should only be legal in the case of rape, incest, or to save the life of the mother.
Abortion should be banned after 12 weeks of pregnancy. After 12 weeks, abortion should only be legal in the case of rape, incest, or to save the life of the mother.
Abortion should be banned after 6 weeks of pregnancy. After 6 weeks, abortion should only be legal in the case of rape, incest, or to save the life of the mother.
The abortion question should be left to the states to decide
All abortions should be banned nationwide to protect the lives of the unborn

Criminal justice.label: Criminal justice
Criminal justice.question: Which is more important in the United States today: keeping sentences harsh to reduce crime, or limiting the length of sentences to reduce mass incarceration?

options.label: Keeping sentences harsh to reduce crime
Limiting the length of sentences to reduce mass incarceration

Trans sports_1.label: Trans sports_1
Trans sports_1.question: When it comes to transgender women and girls — that is, people whose biological sex is male but who identify as female — do you think transgender women and girls should or should not be allowed to compete with other women and girls at each of the following levels? - Professional sports

Trans sports_2.label: Trans sports_2
Trans sports_2.question: When it comes to transgender women and girls — that is, people whose biological sex is male but who identify as female — do you think transgender women and girls should or should not be allowed to compete with other women and girls at each of the following levels? - College sports

Trans sports_4.question: When it comes to transgender women and girls — that is, people whose biological sex is male but who identify as female — do you think transgender women and girls should or should not be allowed to compete with other women and girls at each of the following levels? - Youth sports

Youth transition_1.label: Youth transition_1
Youth transition_1.question: When it comes to gender transition treatments — such as hormone therapy or gender transition surgery — when is the youngest age you think people should or should not be allowed to receive these treatments? - People 21 and older

options.label: Should be allowed, regardless of parental consent
Should be allowed only with parental consent

Youth transition_2.label: Youth transition_2
Youth transition_2.question: When it comes to gender transition treatments — such as hormone therapy or gender transition surgery — when is the youngest age you think people should or should not be allowed to receive these treatments? - People between the ages of 18 and 21

options.label: Should be allowed, regardless of parental consent
Should be allowed only with parental consent

Youth transition_3.label: Youth transition_3
Youth transition_3.question: When it comes to gender transition treatments — such as hormone therapy or gender transition surgery — when is the youngest age you think people should or should not be allowed to receive these treatments? - People between the ages of 13 and 17

options.label: Should be allowed, regardless of parental consent
Should be allowed only with parental consent

Youth transition_4.label: Youth transition_4
Youth transition_4.question: When it comes to gender transition treatments — such as hormone therapy or gender transition surgery — when is the youngest age you think people should or should not be allowed to receive these treatments? - People 13 and under

options.label: Should be allowed, regardless of parental consent
Should be allowed only with parental consent

PC 1_1.label: PC 1_1
PC 1_1.question: Do you agree or disagree with the following: - Democrats are too aggressive at policing language, which threatens free speech

PC 1_2.label: PC 1_2
PC 1_2.question: Do you agree or disagree with the following: - I would rather have a beer with a Republican politician than a Democratic one

PC 1_3.label: PC 1_3
PC 1_3.question: Do you agree or disagree with the following: - Republicans don’t do enough to make sure that their supporters don’t say offensive things

PC 2.label: PC 2
PC 2.question: Which comes closer to your view:

options.label: Words have impacts. We have to ensure the way we talk is respectful towards women, people of color and other minority groups so they feel welcome in workplaces and other public spaces.
Words are just words. The impulse to censor language is destroying our culture and the careers of good people who said something dumb or a perfectly innocent comment that was misinterpreted

Weed legalization.label: Weed legalization
Weed legalization.question: When it comes to marijuana legalization, which of the following comes closest to your view?

options.label: Marijuana should be legalized federally for recreational and medicinal use
Marijuana should be legalized federally for medicinal use only
Marijuana legalization should be left to the states

Vaccine mandates.label: Vaccine mandates
Vaccine mandates.question: In your opinion, which of the following comes closest to what should be the vaccination requirement for public schools?

options.label: All students should be required to be vaccinated
All students should be required to be vaccinated, except for those with medical conditions preventing them from being vaccinated
All students should be vaccinated, but exemptions should be granted for medical, religious, and philosophical reasons
There should be no vaccination requirements

Homelessness A.label: Homelessness A
Homelessness A.question: When it comes to homelessness, which position comes closest to your view?

options.label: Housing is a human right, and the way to fix homelessness is for the government to guarantee  housing to the poor
Homeless people are often drug addicts or mentally ill, and they need to be taken off the streets and locked up to protect law-abiding citizens

Homelessness B.label: Homelessness B
Homelessness B.question: When it comes to homelessness, which position comes closest to your view?

options.label: Our response to homelessness should focus on helping people find shelter and treatment, not on throwing poor people in jail
Homeless people are often drug addicts or mentally ill, and they need to be taken off the streets and locked up to protect law-abiding citizens

Uni statements.label: Uni statements
Uni statements.question:
To what extent do you agree with the following statement? 

Universities should publish statements that take a position in response to current political and social issues.

Teacher disclosure.label: Teacher disclosure
Teacher disclosure.question: Which view aligns closest to your stance on children who identify as LGBTQ+ in schools?

options.label: Teachers and other staff should be required to disclose a student’s gender identity or sexual orientation to the student’s parents/guardians.
Teachers should be forbidden from disclosing a student’s gender identity or sexual orientation to the student’s parents/guardian without the student’s permission.
Teachers should use their best judgement in determining whether to disclose a student’s gender identity or sexual orientation to the student’s parents/guardian.

Standardized tests.label: Standardized tests
Standardized tests.question: When it comes to standardized tests like the SAT or ACT, which of the following comes closest to your view?

options.label: Standardized tests aren’t perfect, but overall they are fair and make college admissions more meritocratic

Test optional.label: Test optional
Test optional.question: Which of the following comes closest to your views about the use of standardized testing in college admissions?

options.label: Universities should require all applicants to submit standardized test scores
Universities should allow applicants to choose whether or not to submit test scores (also known as test-optional admissions)
Universities should not accept standardized test scores from any applicants

Ivy League favs.label: Ivy League favs
Ivy League favs.question: In general, how would you say you feel about elite private universities, such as the Ivy League?

Higher ed trust_1.label: Higher ed trust_1
Higher ed trust_1.question: How much would you say that you trust professors from the following kinds of higher education institutions to educate America’s youth? - Elite private universities, like the Ivy League

Higher ed trust_2.label: Higher ed trust_2
Higher ed trust_2.question: How much would you say that you trust professors from the following kinds of higher education institutions to educate America’s youth? - Private non-profit universities

Higher ed trust_3.label: Higher ed trust_3
Higher ed trust_3.question: How much would you say that you trust professors from the following kinds of higher education institutions to educate America’s youth? - Public 4-year universities

Higher ed trust_4.label: Higher ed trust_4
Higher ed trust_4.question: How much would you say that you trust professors from the following kinds of higher education institutions to educate America’s youth? - Community colleges

Higher ed trust_5.label: Higher ed trust_5
Higher ed trust_5.question: How much would you say that you trust professors from the following kinds of higher education institutions to educate America’s youth? - Private for-profit colleges

Higher ed trust_6.label: Higher ed trust_6
Higher ed trust_6.question: How much would you say that you trust professors from the following kinds of higher education institutions to educate America’s youth? - Trade schools

Pell Grant MGP.label: Pell Grant MGP
Pell Grant MGP.question: Federal Pell Grants help low-income students afford college. Currently, Pell Grants can only be used for four-year colleges and graduate schools. Would you support changing the law so that Pell Grants could be used for trade schools?

Endowment taxes.label: Endowment taxes
Endowment taxes.question: To what extent would you support having private universities with an endowment — a financial fund made up of donations to a university, typically invested to support the institution’s operations, scholarships, faculty salaries, and research — greater than $10 billion paying a tax of 21% on the annual profits from their endowments? Under current law, profits from university endowments are taxed at 1.4%.

Your econ priority.label: Your econ priority
Your econ priority.question: If you could only improve one aspect of the American economy, which of the following would you choose?

options.label: Lower interest rates on credit cards and mortgages
Lower prices on goods, services, and gas (lower inflation)

GOP econ priority.label: GOP econ priority
GOP econ priority.question: Using the same list, which aspect of the American economy do you think the Republican Party is most focused on?

options.label: Lower interest rates on credit cards and mortgages
Lower prices on goods, services, and gas (lower inflation)

Dem econ priority.label: Dem econ priority
Dem econ priority.question: Using the same list, which aspect of the American economy do you think the Democratic Party is most focused on?

options.label: Lower interest rates on credit cards and mortgages
Lower prices on goods, services, and gas (lower inflation)

Budget perceptions_6.question: For every $100 in tax revenue the US federal government receives, how much do you think goes to: - Foreign aid

Budget perceptions_8.question: For every $100 in tax revenue the US federal government receives, how much do you think goes to: - Welfare programs

Budget perceptions_9.question: For every $100 in tax revenue the US federal government receives, how much do you think goes to: - Infrastructure and transportation

Sports betting.label: Sports betting
Sports betting.question: When it comes to the legalization of online sports gambling, which comes closer to your view?

options.label: This is a free country and if Americans want to bet on sports they should be allowed to. Sports betting raises tax revenue for education, and we shouldn’t restrict the freedom of the vast majority of responsible players just because some people make bad choices. Online sports betting should be legal.

Sports betting is a predatory industry because companies depend on gambling addicts to make profits. Research has found that young working-class men are the most likely to become addicted, leading to higher rates of bankruptcy and debt. And constant gambling ads ruin the experience of watching sports. Online sports betting should not be legal.

Lina Khan.label: Lina Khan
Lina Khan.question: When it comes to antitrust policy — that is, policies meant to ensure fair competition between companies — which comes closer to your view?

options.label: Antitrust policy should be mainly focused on keeping consumer prices low, even if that sometimes means not breaking up large companies
Antitrust policy should be mainly focused on keeping corporate power in check, even if that sometimes means higher prices

Crypto favs.label: Crypto favs
Crypto favs.question: How do you feel about cryptocurrencies generally?

Nuclear support.label: Nuclear support
Nuclear support.question: Overall, do you strongly favor, somewhat favor, somewhat oppose or strongly oppose the use of nuclear energy as one of the ways to provide electricity for the U.S.?

Nuclear expand.label: Nuclear expand
Nuclear expand.question: When it comes to the use of nuclear energy to provide electricity, which of the following comes closest to your view?

options.label: America should invest more in nuclear energy to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and combat climate change
America should invest more in nuclear energy to lower electricity bills for consumers
America should not invest more in nuclear energy, because it is dangerous for the environment

Deficit reduction A.label: Deficit reduction A
Deficit reduction A.question: When it comes to reducing the deficit — the shortfall between what the government spends and what it collects in revenue each year, requiring borrowing to cover the gap — which approach do you prefer the most?

options.label: Reduce the deficit mainly by cutting government spending
Reduce the deficit mainly by raising taxes
Reduce the deficit with a combination of tax hikes and spending cuts
Don’t think deficit reduction is a priority

Tax hikes A_1.label: Tax hikes A_1
Tax hikes A_1.question: What do you think of the following types of taxes? - Wealth and high-income taxes (e.g., taxes on billionaires, millionaires, and income over $400,000)

options.label: Should be kept at the same level

Tax hikes A_2.label: Tax hikes A_2
Tax hikes A_2.question: What do you think of the following types of taxes? - Middle- and low-income taxes (e.g., income taxes on people making less than $100,000)

options.label: Should be kept at the same level

Tax hikes A_3.label: Tax hikes A_3
Tax hikes A_3.question: What do you think of the following types of taxes? - Business taxes (e.g., corporate taxes, payroll taxes)

options.label: Should be kept at the same level

Tax hikes A_4.question: What do you think of the following types of taxes? - Environmental taxes (e.g., taxes on greenhouse gas emissions, or other pollution)

options.label: Should be kept at the same level

Tax hikes A_5.question: What do you think of the following types of taxes? - Consumption taxes (e.g., excise taxes on cigarettes/alcohol, sales taxes)

options.label: Should be kept at the same level

Tax hikes A_6.label: Tax hikes A_6
Tax hikes A_6.question: What do you think of the following types of taxes? - Taxes on imported goods (tariffs)

options.label: Should be kept at the same level

Tariffs general.label: Tariffs general
Tariffs general.question: Tariffs are a tax on imported goods, paid for by the person or company who imports the goods. Donald Trump has proposed new tariffs on all imports to the United States, arguing that they will reduce the national debt and create jobs. Democrats support tariffs on some strategic goods—such as microchips and electric vehicles—to prevent adversaries like China from gaining a competitive edge. Economists warn that any tariffs could hurt the economy by raising prices for consumers, which must be weighed against potential national security benefits. Which of the following best reflects your view?

options.label: America should impose blanket tariffs on all imports in order to boost American manufacturing
America should impose strategic tariffs on only some goods, so that we can compete with China
America should try to avoid tariffs so we don’t make inflation worse

Immigration level.label: Immigration level
Immigration level.question: When it comes to legal immigration to the US, which comes closest to your view?

options.label: Legal immigration should be kept at its present level

Mass deportations_1.label: Mass deportations_1
Mass deportations_1.question: Below are descriptions of various categories of illegal immigrants to the United States. For each, please select whether you think they should or should not be deported. - Someone who crossed the US border illegally and then claimed refugee or asylum status

Mass deportations_2.label: Mass deportations_2
Mass deportations_2.question: Below are descriptions of various categories of illegal immigrants to the United States. For each, please select whether you think they should or should not be deported. - Someone brought to the US illegally as a child and is now an adult (also known as DREAMers)

Mass deportations_3.label: Mass deportations_3
Mass deportations_3.question: Below are descriptions of various categories of illegal immigrants to the United States. For each, please select whether you think they should or should not be deported. - Someone who came to the US illegally as an adult and now has children who are US citizens

Mass deportations_4.label: Mass deportations_4
Mass deportations_4.question: Below are descriptions of various categories of illegal immigrants to the United States. For each, please select whether you think they should or should not be deported. - Someone who illegally entered the US over 10 years ago and has no criminal record

Mass deportations_5.label: Mass deportations_5
Mass deportations_5.question: Below are descriptions of various categories of illegal immigrants to the United States. For each, please select whether you think they should or should not be deported. - Someone who illegally entered the US and has been convicted of a violent crime

Mass deportations_6.label: Mass deportations_6
Mass deportations_6.question: Below are descriptions of various categories of illegal immigrants to the United States. For each, please select whether you think they should or should not be deported. - Someone who illegally entered the US and has been convicted of a non-violent crime, such as selling illegal drugs or shoplifting

Student deportations.label: Student deportations
Student deportations.question:
You may have heard about some international students who participated in campus protests against the war in Gaza being detained by federal immigration authorities and threatened with deportation. 

White House officials argue that these students should be deported, or have their visas revoked, because their actions in the protests were antisemitic and constituted a threat to national security under a 1952 law. 

Legal experts say that these detentions are unconstitutional and threaten free speech, since the Supreme Court has ruled in the past that the First Amendment applies to non-citizens. 

Which of the following comes closer to your view?

options.label: International students should be deported for participating in campus protests
International students should not be deported for participating in campus protests

Ukraine.label: Ukraine
Ukraine.question: When it comes to the Ukraine conflict, which position comes closest to your view?

options.label: America should stop providing aid to Ukraine, negotiate a peace deal with Russia to stop the war, and focus our resources on issues at home
America should continue to provide aid to Ukraine so they can stand up to Putin’s invasion and protect their right to decide their own country’s future

Middle East.label: Middle East
Middle East.question: When it comes to the Middle East, which comes closest to your view?

options.label: America should increase its involvement in the Middle East. A larger American presence is needed to combat terrorism and keep bad actors like Iran in check.

America should maintain its current level of involvement in the Middle East. It is important to continue our counter-terrorism operations and ensure regional stability for allied countries, like Israel.

America should reduce its involvement in the Middle East. The region’s problems are not fixable with more US involvement, and we should be focusing our attention on competing with China in the Pacific and Asia.

Taiwan.label: Taiwan
Taiwan.question: When it comes to China and Taiwan, which position comes closest to your view?

options.label: If China invades Taiwan, America should intervene militarily to protect Taiwan. Taiwan is a democracy, and China shouldn’t be given a blank check to wage wars of conquest whenever it pleases.
If China invades Taiwan, America should provide military and humanitarian aid to Taiwan, but should rule out military intervention.
If China invades Taiwan, America should not militarily intervene or provide economic aid to protect Taiwan. Intervention would risk starting a war with another nuclear power.

Senate control.label: Senate control
Senate control.question: Which party currently holds a majority of the seats in the U.S. Senate?

House control.label: House control
House control.question: Which party currently holds a majority of the seats in the U.S. House of Representatives?

SCOTUS control.label: SCOTUS control
SCOTUS control.question: Which party appointed a majority of the justices currently on the Supreme Court?

SCOTUS appointments.label: SCOTUS appointments
SCOTUS appointments.question: Which description of how judges, including Supreme Court justices, are appointed is most accurate?

options.label: Presidents nominate judges, who must then be confirmed by a simple majority vote in the Senate before taking office
Presidents appoint judges, with no input from the Senate or House
The House and Senate vote to appoint judges by a two-thirds supermajority, with no input from the president

Gerrymandering def..label: Gerrymandering def.
Gerrymandering def..question: Which description of “gerrymandering” is the most accurate?

options.label: When politicians redraw districts for Congress or state legislatures to advantage one political party
Whenever districts for Congress or state legislatures are redrawn
Attempts to suppress the votes of racial minorities or people who are likely to vote for the other party

EC mechanics.label: EC mechanics
EC mechanics.question: Which statement best describes how the Electoral College works in US presidential elections?

options.label: Each state gets a certain number of electoral votes based on how many members it has in Congress (both the House and the Senate). In most states, the candidate with the most votes wins all of that state’s electoral votes. To become president, a candidate needs 270 electoral votes.

Each state gets electoral votes equal to the total number of its members in the House and Senate. In most states, the candidate with the most votes gets all of that state’s electoral votes. The candidate who gets the most electoral votes wins the presidency, even if they don’t have a majority.

Each state gets electoral votes based on the total number of its members in the House and Senate. If one candidate wins the most votes nationwide, they become president. But if no one wins a majority of the popular votes, then the candidate with the most electoral votes wins.

Amendment mechanics.label: Amendment mechanics
Amendment mechanics.question: Which description of how the process to amend the Constitution works is the most accurate?

options.label: First, either two-thirds of both the US House and Senate must vote to propose the amendment to the states, or two-thirds of state legislatures can vote to call a constitutional convention. Then, three-quarters of state legislatures must vote in favor of adopting the amendment.

After Congress passes a bill proposing the amendment, the governors of the fifty states must vote to ratify the amendment before it takes effect. If even one state’s governor votes against the amendment, it does not take effect.
It is not possible to amend the Constitution, as new amendments were banned.

Filibuster.label: Filibuster
Filibuster.question: Which description of the Senate filibuster is most accurate?

options.label: The filibuster is a rule created by the Senate, meaning it could be changed at any time. It says that most legislation needs 60 votes to pass, except for certain budget bills and judicial nominations.
The filibuster is a part of the Constitution that requires 60 votes in the Senate to pass any legislation.
The filibuster is a rule created by the Senate, meaning it could be changed at any time, and says that all legislation needs 60 votes to pass.

Judicial power.label: Judicial power
Judicial power.question: Which view comes closest to your stance on the role of the judiciary?

options.label: The judiciary branch shouldn’t be allowed to control the executive's power. If a judge can unilaterally block every presidential action nationwide, elections become meaningless.

The judiciary branch should have the power to strike down unconstitutional decisions made by the executive branch. The Constitution created a system of checks and balances, and the president should follow court decisions.

SCOTUS bias.label: SCOTUS bias
SCOTUS bias.question: Which is a better description of how the Supreme Court has been ruling on cases, in practice?

options.label: More often than not, the Supreme Court rules on the legal and constitutional merits of cases, without concern for party politics
More often than not, the Supreme Court rules to advantage one political party or ideological group, rather than on the legal or constitutional merits of the case

Money vs ideology.label: Money vs ideology
Money vs ideology.question: What do you think is a bigger problem in politics these days?

options.label: Activist groups who push their ideological agendas on policymakers, even if those ideas are too extreme for most Americans
Wealthy donors who use campaign contributions and lobbyists to push for policies that help them at the expense of the middle class

NPV.label: NPV
NPV.question: When it comes to how the United States elects presidents, which approach do you prefer?

options.label: The United States should switch to a system where the president is elected by a national popular vote. America’s commander-in-chief should be the person who wins the most votes.

The United States should continue with the Electoral College system, even if that means someone can win the presidency while losing the popular vote. This was the system that the Founding Fathers designed, and America should stick with it.

Zoning.label: Zoning
Zoning.question: When it comes to detailed rules about what can be built in your city, which would you prefer?

options.label: Having regular voters decide through ballot measures
Having city planners and experts decide

Confidence in voters_1.label: Confidence in voters_1
Confidence in voters_1.question: Do you agree or disagree with the following statements? - Voters are informed enough to make decisions about politics.

Confidence in voters_2.label: Confidence in voters_2
Confidence in voters_2.question: Do you agree or disagree with the following statements? - I trust elected officials to make decisions.

Confidence in voters_3.label: Confidence in voters_3
Confidence in voters_3.question: Do you agree or disagree with the following statements? - We should have more direct ballot measures where regular voters make decisions instead of elected officials.

Threat MaxDiff 1.label: Threat MaxDiff 1
Threat MaxDiff 1.question: Which of the following issues do you think poses a bigger threat to our democracy?

Threat MaxDiff 2.label: Threat MaxDiff 2
Threat MaxDiff 2.question: Which of the following issues do you think poses a bigger threat to our democracy?

Reform MaxDiff 1.label: Reform MaxDiff 1
Reform MaxDiff 1.question: Which proposal for a democratic reform do you think will improve our government the most?

Reform MaxDiff 2.label: Reform MaxDiff 2
Reform MaxDiff 2.question: Which proposal for a democratic reform do you think will improve our government the most?
